frameworks.name: deep neural network
frameworks.one_line: hierarchical DNN repurposed as a computational model of visual perceptual learning

frameworks.name: visual perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: DNN extends classical readout models with multi-layer plasticity over training

core_question: Can a deep neural network serve as a unifying multi-level model of visual perceptual learning?
key_assumptions: VPL involves plasticity distributed across multiple visual processing stages
a generic DNN's hierarchical units can stand in for cortical visual areas
Gabor orientation discrimination is a representative VPL task
manipulations: task precision of Gabor orientation discrimination during DNN training
stimulus conditions for testing transfer to coarse discriminations
key_findings: DNN reproduced increased specificity with higher task precision
learning precise discriminations transferred asymmetrically to coarse ones when stimulus conditions varied
distribution of plasticity shifted toward lower layers as task precision increased
learned unit changes resembled monkey visual-area electrophysiology
interpretations: DNNs provide a sufficiently rich substrate to link behavioral VPL and multi-area plasticity
precision of the task governs where in the hierarchy learning is allocated
